findings.finding: The RP00 contract validator and the synthetic fixture derive their 'expected' arrays (forbidden_permission_claims, fail_closed_on, decision_values, permission_scopes, result_fields, etc.) from the very policy module they are meant to validate. This makes contract<->fixture<->module comparisons self-referential: a semantically wrong but internally consistent constant (e.g., a typo in permission_decision_ref or an omitted-but-never-listed dangerous claim) would pass every automated gate. Coverage here depends on human review (this review) plus the hard-coded literal expectations in assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPermissionPrecheckResult.
findings.evidence: scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs: comparisons such as ['forbidden_permission_claims', testAndGoldenPermissionPolicyModule?.forbiddenPermissionClaims] and ['result_fields', expectedTestAndGoldenPermissionResultFields] use the module as both source and expectation; packages/control-plane/src/validators.js pins literals independently, which is the real backstop.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout. Continue to rely on the literal expectations embedded in validators.js (which are independent of policies.js) as the authoritative anti-drift backstop, and keep pack-level human review in the loop for new constants.

findings.finding: The RP00 validator's 'denied Matter trace' negative case builds the input by spreading the allow-path Matter-trace fixture result and overriding only matter_trace_satisfied/matter_trace_decision/blocked_claim_refs, leaving matter_trace_required=false. The resulting object is internally inconsistent (required=false yet satisfied=false). If assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetMatterTracePrecheckResult enforces cross-field consistency, the throw may originate from the shape assertion rather than the intended explicit allow_metadata_only/satisfied gate, so the test exercises the gate less precisely than its label implies. The parallel service.test.js case uses a genuine denied result (via touches_golden_case_with_matter_data) and is precise; both still correctly assert a throw, so behavior is fail-closed either way.
findings.evidence: scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs negative table entry ["denied Matter trace", { ...fixtureTestAndGoldenMatterTrace?.result, matter_trace_satisfied: false, matter_trace_decision: "deny_blocked_claim_required", blocked_claim_refs: [...] }, {}]; vs. packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js createTestAndGoldenCaseSetMatterTraceResult({}, { touches_golden_case_with_matter_data: true }).
findings.recommendation: Optional: align the RP00 validator's denied case with a genuinely consistent denied trace (or set matter_trace_required:true) so the negative test provably hits the permission-decision gate. No closeout impact.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-033 adds the metadata-only Permission precheck stage RP00.P02.M07.S05 for the Test And Golden Case Set entrypoint. It introduces CONTROL_PLANE_TEST_AND_GOLDEN_CASE_SET_PERMISSION_PRECHECK_{RESULT_FIELDS,POLICY}, the CONTROL_PLANE_TEST_AND_GOLDEN_CASE_SET_PERMISSION_PRECHECKER module, getControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPermissionPrecheckPolicy, precheckControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPermission, and assert{Policy,Result} validators, wired consistently through the contract definition, synthetic fixture, README, and RP00 validator. The implementation is a pure function that consumes the carried RP00.P02.M07.S04 Matter-trace result, requires a fixed metadata permission_decision_ref (allow_metadata_only), and emits a frozen result with every runtime/AuthZ/permission-engine/security-trimming/audit-write/test-execution/golden-generation/golden-persistence/service-logic/runtime-route/database/storage/product-state flag set to false. It performs no I/O, no permission evaluation, and no writes. Fail-closed handling is comprehensive: it rejects missing/denied/wrong-entrypoint/wrong-marker/wrong-source Matter-trace inputs, non-object claims, and ANY truthy permission claim (28 enumerated forbidden claims plus a catch-all unknown-claim rejection). It correctly defers the audit-hint precheck to RP00.P02.M07.S06 and does not complete RP00.P02.M07. The pack matches the already-closed sibling pattern (Synthetic Fixture Set permission precheck) with entrypoint-appropriate additions (test-execution and golden-fixture boundaries; catalog/unit-test mutation forbidden claims). All field-by-field consistency between policy, service result, validator, contract definition, fixture, and RP00 validator checks out, consistent with the reported passing of node --check, node --test (504/504), npm test (588/588), npm run validate, spec/weighted/fullplan validators. The only outstanding item is the intentionally-deferred docs/closeout-packs/cp00-033 evidence pack, which is the expected next closeout step and the sole reason rp00:control-plane:validate currently fails. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.
parsed_review.residual_risk: 1) Closeout gate: rp00:control-plane:validate intentionally still fails until docs/closeout-packs/cp00-033 (command-evidence + construction-inspection asserting control_plane_test_and_golden_case_set_permission_precheck_verified:true, plus adjudication) is created; this evidence pack must exist and the validator must pass before CP00-033 can close. 2) Declarative boundaries: all 'no runtime permission evaluation / AuthZ / permission engine / security trimming / audit write / test execution / golden generation/persistence / runtime route / service / database / storage / product-state write' guarantees are metadata assertions on a pure function emitting *_permitted=false; they are correct for this contract-first phase (no runtime exists), but provide no protection against a future runtime that ignores them — enforcement must be re-verified when service logic is implemented. 3) Forward constraint: matter_id is hard-pinned to null in both precheckControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPermission and assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPermissionPrecheckResult; this is consistent with sibling packs and explicitly documented, but the result validator will reject any future Matter-scoped (non-null matter_id) input and must be revisited when Matter-scoped inputs are introduced. 4) The pack does not modify any other entrypoint, does not complete RP00.P02.M07/RP00.P02, and does not touch H00/C00 authority; scope containment verified against the 8 changed files only.

adjudication_notes: Claude approved CP00-033 with only P3 findings. F1 is non-blocking because the accepted pattern intentionally treats non-false claim values as set claims and fails closed rather than authorizing behavior. F2 is non-blocking because contract fixtures are generated from live policy/service outputs and final command evidence records service, full test, RP00, pack, and product validators after evidence creation.
